title: Today's Tech Brief: YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos
summary: YouTube's new AI labeling, shadow AI use by workers, and more tech news
imageAlt: Image from source article for YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos
imageCaption: YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos
imageSourceTitle: YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. I've got a few stories to share today that caught my eye in the world of technology. YouTube is stepping up its game when it comes to transparency around AI-generated content.

First off, YouTube will start automatically labeling videos that have been created or heavily edited using AI. This move aims to provide more clarity for viewers, especially as AI-generated content becomes more prevalent. According to a report from Ars Technica, this change is part of YouTube's effort to be more upfront about how content is created.

Next, I've been reading about how some bosses might be a bit too confident when it comes to their workers' use of AI tools. An Okta survey found that more than half of organizations faced an AI-related security incident or near miss last year. It seems like some companies might not be as on top of 'shadow AI' use by their employees as they think.

Also on the board, I came across another interesting piece about the growing use of AI in content creation. With tools like Google's new Omni model, the lines between what's real and what's generated by AI are getting blurrier by the day. YouTube's move to label AI videos is a step towards keeping things transparent.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

Bosses blinded by confidence about shadow AI use by workers
